healthy.summary: Plant looks healthy — no treatment needed.

early_blight.summary: Early blight (Alternaria solani). Remove affected leaves and apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (or an alternative such as Kumir, Snow shrf, or Antracol) on a 7–10 day schedule.

late_blight.summary: Late blight (Phytophthora infestans). Act fast — apply Metalaxyl + Mancozeb (or Kumir, Snow shrf, or Antracol) and destroy heavily infected plants.

healthy.summary: Plant looks healthy — no treatment needed.

early_blight.summary: Early blight. Prune lower leaves and apply Antracol, Metalaxyl + Mancozeb, or Kumir on a weekly schedule.

late_blight.summary: Late blight. Apply Antracol, Metalaxyl + Mancozeb, or Kumir immediately and remove infected foliage.

leaf_mold.summary: Leaf mold (Passalora fulva). Improve ventilation and apply Wettable Sulphur.

septoria_leaf_spot.summary: Septoria leaf spot. Remove infected leaves and apply copper or chlorothalonil fungicide.

bacterial_spot.summary: Bacterial spot. Apply copper-based bactericide; rotate crops next season.

spider_mites.summary: Two-spotted spider mites. Spray with Abamectin, Nemesis, or Magnum Gold; rinse foliage to dislodge mites.

target_spot.summary: Target spot (Corynespora cassiicola). Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide and remove infected lower leaves.

yellow_leaf_curl_virus.summary: Tomato Yellow Leaf Curl Virus — spread by whiteflies. Remove infected plants and control whiteflies with imidacloprid or insecticidal soap.

mosaic_virus.summary: Tomato mosaic virus — no chemical cure. Remove infected plants, disinfect tools, and avoid handling plants after tobacco.

healthy.summary: Plant looks healthy — no treatment needed.

common_rust.summary: Common rust (Puccinia sorghi). Apply Tebuconazole or Shavit if infection is heavy.

northern_leaf_blight.summary: Northern leaf blight (Exserohilum turcicum). Apply Azoxystrobin or Copper Oxychloride; rotate crops.

gray_leaf_spot.summary: Gray leaf spot (Cercospora zeae-maydis). Apply Triadimenol and improve residue management.
